parts.text: i shipped my first pygame loop in 2020
. now i am working to solve hard problems across any domain with multimodal ai
i'm studying cs @ Rutgers. as a swe i've worked across devops
, etc. seemingly unrelated surfaces but the same problem sphere: the intersection of ai research and product.
i learn by building, so i have a lot of
can't debug my slop, i like to

references.1: Started with Tkinter turtle, then PyGame, then HTML5 Canvas. Returned to Python with much more ambitious ideas in AI.
references.2: VLMs, MLMs, SLMs. Deep-diving in VLMs right now.
references.3: Raw video, EHR notes, voice, logs — where most of the real signal actually lives.
references.4: Cloud migration @ Datanexxt; infra & LLM APIs @ Undergraduation.com. Scalable systems are a must-know.
references.5: 3E8 Robotics — autonomous multi-floor delivery; elevator navigation, payload handoff. Backed by Founders Inc., based in SF. Also: deformables & generative serial chains as side work.
references.6: Clinical transformers & PK/PD modeling @ Centren Health, NYC. Osler — RAG onboarding for hospitalists, 3rd place @ RUHealthHack.
references.7: Product eng @ Undergraduation.com. Stem In Place — 501(c)(3) free online STEM summer courses, 1500 students, 40 instructors. Also taught @ Code Ninjas & Bricks 4 Kidz.
references.8: Incoming @ Fiserv. Possibilities Summit @ Goldman Sachs. SWE virtual intern @ JPMorgan Chase. Mesh — 1st place Rutgers x Fiserv hackathon.
references.9: Never took a tutorial. What I've made is what I know.
references.11: Updating irregularly since summer 2021 — AI (tech & philosophy), programming & life.
references.12: Completely offline. Mainly Camelback in the Poconos.
references.13: There's something satisfying about building things with your own hands.
references.14: White rice, black beans, chicken al pastor ×2, tomatillo red chili, roasted chili corn, cheese, light sour cream.
